title: Coelogyne lurida
intro: Coelogyne lurida, a species of orchid, is a flowering plant known for its unique yellowish-green flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne lurida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as flavonols and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne lurida is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Coelogyne lurida is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is classified under the genus Coelogyne. This genus is further divided into several sections, with Coelogyne lurida being part of the section Coelogyne. Coelogyne lurida belongs to the tribe Coelogyneae, which is one of the many tribes within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.
uses: Coelogyne lurida has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne lurida is used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Its bioactive compounds may also have potential in the development of new medicines. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal applications and potential side effects.
